title: EntityMapper Pattern ŌĆö NxGN Actions
purpose: **AI Summary**: Entity mappers are DI-registered classes that transform inbound DTOs to entities. The mapper is a **pure transformer** ŌĆö it resolves existing entities (by ID, then business key fallback), determines create vs update, maps DTO fields to entity properties, sets audit fields, handles translations via `TranslationService`, and returns `EntityMapperResult<T>`. It contains NO business logic, NO guards, NO cross-entity operations, and NEVER returns OneOf/Conflict/NotFound. All busine...

examples.name: Core Principle: Pure Transformer Example 1
examples.code:
EntityMapper = DTO ŌåÆ Entity field mapping + entity resolution (find or create) + translations
Command      = Business rules + guards + cross-entity ops + SaveChangesAsync

The mapper NEVER makes business decisions.
The command NEVER maps DTO fields to entity properties.

examples.code:
Features/{Entity}/Shared/Mappers/
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ {Entity}EntityMapper.cs         # MapAsync() ŌåÆ EntityMapperResult<T>
ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ {Entity}DTOMapper.cs            # Map(), MapGridItem(), MapLookup()

rules.rule_text: Return `EntityMapperResult<T>` ŌĆö never OneOf, never Conflict, never NotFound
Use batch-primary signature: `MapAsync(List<DTO> dtoList, Guid userId, Guid requestLanguageId, Guid defaultLanguageId, CancellationToken ct, ActionsDbContext? db = null)`
Provide single-DTO convenience overload that wraps in a list
Accept `CancellationToken ct` and propagate to all DB calls
Accept optional `ActionsDbContext? db = null` for shared context
Use `Guid.CreateVersion7()` for new entity IDs
Detect create vs update with `dto.Id == Guid.Empty`
Set `CreatedDate`/`CreatedById` on create, `UpdatedDate`/`UpdatedById` on update
Handle translations via `TranslationService.AddOrUpdateEntityTranslation`
Map scalar fields only ŌĆö child collections belong in the Command
Dispose DbContext only if the mapper created it
Return `OneOf`, `Conflict`, `NotFound`, or any error type ŌĆö the mapper is a pure transformer
Contain business logic (guards, permission checks, cross-entity validation)
Create entities of a different type than `T` (e.g., mapper for `ActionEntity` must not create `WorkflowStep`)
Call `SaveChangesAsync` ŌĆö the Command handles persistence
Validate business rules ŌĆö that belongs in validators or commands
Accept `tenantId` as a parameter ŌĆö RLS handles tenant scoping
Use business key fallback lookup when `Guid.Empty` is provided and entity has a unique business key
Comment immutable fields that are intentionally omitted from the update path
Set entity defaults (e.g., `IsActive = true`) in the create path for fields not on the DTO

sections.title: Core Principle: Pure Transformer
content: EntityMapper = DTO ŌåÆ Entity field mapping + entity resolution (find or create) + translations
Command      = Business rules + guards + cross-entity ops + SaveChangesAsync
The mapper NEVER makes business decisions.
The command NEVER maps DTO fields to entity properties.

Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ For each dto in dtoList:
Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ dto.Id == Guid.Empty ŌåÆ LOOKUP BY BUSINESS KEY (if applicable)
Ōöé   Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ Found by business key ŌåÆ UPDATE (upsert)
Ōöé   Ōöé   Ōöé   Apply DTO fields to existing entity
Ōöé   Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ Not found ŌåÆ CREATE
Ōöé   Ōöé       New entity with Guid.CreateVersion7()
Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ dto.Id != Guid.Empty ŌåÆ LOOKUP BY ID
Ōöé   Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ Found by ID ŌåÆ UPDATE
Ōöé   Ōöé   Ōöé   Apply DTO fields to existing entity
Ōöé   Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ Not found by ID ŌåÆ Return empty result
Ōöé   Ōöé       (Command detects empty result ŌåÆ handles accordingly)
Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ Continue to next dto

sections.title: Mapper vs Command Boundary
content: This boundary is the most important rule in the pattern. Violations create inconsistency across the codebase and make mappers untestable.
| DTO to Entity field mapping | **Mapper** | Pure data transformation |
| Entity resolution (ID lookup, business key fallback) | **Mapper** | Determines create vs update |
| Audit fields (CreatedDate/CreatedById, UpdatedDate/UpdatedById) | **Mapper** | Set from server context, never from DTO |
| Entity defaults (e.g., `IsActive = true`, `Status = Active`) | **Mapper** | Set on create for fields not on the DTO |
| Immutable field handling (skip on update, set on create) | **Mapper** | Comment why field is omitted on update |
| Translation handling (AddOrUpdateEntityTranslation) | **Mapper** | Translations are part of field mapping |
| Business rules (disabled-entity guard, duplicate check) | **Command** | Domain logic, not transformation |
| Cross-entity operations (create related entities) | **Command** | Mapper only maps entities of type T |
| Child collection diffing | **Command** | Aggregate root management |
| Audit logging | **Command** or **Endpoint** | After successful persistence |
| FK validation (does referenced entity exist?) | **Command** | Business rule, not field mapping |
**Test:** If you're writing an `if` statement in a mapper that doesn't relate to "is this field null/empty" or "does this entity exist", it probably belongs in the command.

sections.title: EntityMapperResult<T>
content: // UpdatedItems are already tracked by EF ŌĆö no explicit call needed

sections.title: Standard Signature
content: => MapAsync([dto], userId, requestLanguageId, defaultLanguageId, ct, db);
| `dtoList` | List of SaveRequests (inherits DTO) ŌĆö the inbound data (batch primary) |
| `userId` | User performing the action ŌĆö for audit fields |
| `requestLanguageId` | Caller's preferred language ŌĆö for translations |
| `defaultLanguageId` | System default language ŌĆö for translation fallback |
| `ct` | CancellationToken ŌĆö propagate to all DB calls |
| `db` | Optional shared DbContext ŌĆö mapper disposes only if it created one |
**No `tenantId`** ŌĆö RLS handles tenant scoping. If the mapper needs tenant context for entity creation, the command passes it via the shared DbContext's `EffectiveTenantProvider`.

sections.title: Reference Implementation
content: `ActionEntityMapper` - scalar fields, catalogue codes, immutable fields, translations:
The command validates `ActionTypeCode` against active tenant catalogue records before the mapper runs. The mapper only copies the already accepted value.

sections.title: Lookup Strategy
content: Most Actions entities use ID-only lookup when the client knows the entity ID:
For entities with a unique business key such as slug or code, the mapper can fall back to business key lookup when `Guid.Empty` is provided. This supports upsert-by-business-key:
Business key lookup is entity resolution, not business logic. The mapper determines whether the entity already exists; it does not decide whether the caller may create or update it.

sections.title: SaveRequest Inherits DTO
content: `Guid.Empty` on the inherited `Id` property signals create. The mapper receives the SaveRequest (which IS a DTO) and maps all inherited fields.

sections.title: Translation Handling
content: Actions uses translations from day one. Entities implementing `ITranslatedEntity<T>` have translations set in the mapper via `TranslationService`:
// On create ŌĆö adds a new translation for the request language
// On update ŌĆö updates the translation for the request language
The `TranslationService` is injected via primary constructor. Language IDs (`requestLanguageId`, `defaultLanguageId`) are passed as method parameters.

sections.title: Enum and Catalogue Fields
content: True system enum fields on DTOs use `EnumDTO` ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md)); the mapper reads `Id` and casts:

User-configurable catalogues are not enum fields. For tenant-owned lookup data such as Action Types or RCA Methods, the command validates the submitted `Code` or `Id` against the owning admin module records, and the mapper copies the accepted value:
Do not create compatibility mappers that translate stale enum aliases into catalogue records.

sections.title: Immutable Fields
content: Set on create, intentionally omitted on update with a comment:
// Update: Slug is immutable ŌĆö not mapped on update
The command validates immutability before calling the mapper.

sections.title: Common Mistakes
content: // WRONG ŌĆö Business logic in mapper (throws or returns error)
throw new InvalidOperationException("Cannot modify protected entity");  // ŌåÉ belongs in Command
// WRONG ŌĆö Creating a different entity type in mapper
db.WorkflowSteps.Add(new WorkflowStep { ... });  // ŌåÉ belongs in Command
// WRONG ŌĆö Error types in mapper return (OneOf, CommandResult, etc.)
public async Task<OneOf<EntityMapperResult<T>, NotFound, Conflict>> MapAsync(...)
// CORRECT ŌĆö always EntityMapperResult<T> (errors belong in the Command)
// WRONG ŌĆö Single-DTO only, no batch support
// CORRECT ŌĆö Batch primary, single-DTO convenience overload
public async Task<EntityMapperResult<T>> MapAsync(SaveRequest dto, Guid userId, CancellationToken ct)
// CORRECT ŌĆö Actions has translations from day one

sections.title: Rules
content: 1. Return `EntityMapperResult<T>` ŌĆö never OneOf, never Conflict, never NotFound
2. Use batch-primary signature: `MapAsync(List<DTO> dtoList, Guid userId, Guid requestLanguageId, Guid defaultLanguageId, CancellationToken ct, ActionsDbContext? db = null)`
3. Provide single-DTO convenience overload that wraps in a list
4. Accept `CancellationToken ct` and propagate to all DB calls
5. Accept optional `ActionsDbContext? db = null` for shared context
7. Detect create vs update with `dto.Id == Guid.Empty`
10. Map scalar fields only ŌĆö child collections belong in the Command
11. Dispose DbContext only if the mapper created it
1. Return `OneOf`, `Conflict`, `NotFound`, or any error type ŌĆö the mapper is a pure transformer
2. Contain business logic (guards, permission checks, cross-entity validation)
3. Create entities of a different type than `T` (e.g., mapper for `ActionEntity` must not create `WorkflowStep`)
4. Call `SaveChangesAsync` ŌĆö the Command handles persistence
5. Validate business rules ŌĆö that belongs in validators or commands
6. Accept `tenantId` as a parameter ŌĆö RLS handles tenant scoping
1. Use business key fallback lookup when `Guid.Empty` is provided and entity has a unique business key
2. Comment immutable fields that are intentionally omitted from the update path
3. Set entity defaults (e.g., `IsActive = true`) in the create path for fields not on the DTO
